@documentation.@comment: Abstract base for actions that react to trigger firings. Secrets never live in the database — Action documents reference executor names and logical parameters; credentials come from executor-local env vars. Default mode is approval; auto must be opt-in per action because side effects are not revertible.
Calls an HTTP endpoint when its trigger fires. No auth fields by design — executors read credentials from env (e.g. WEBHOOK_DEFAULT_TOKEN).
Sends a notification through a channel selected by the inherited executor field, e.g. 'notify:gotify'.

Idempotent execution record tying one Action to one TriggerFiring. The Lexical key [action, firing] makes planning idempotent — exactly one execution per (action, firing) pair; renotification/nagging is notification-path behavior handled via TriggerFiring statuses, not multiple executions. There is deliberately NO running status: with a polling single-replica daemon and no lease protocol a running state would strand executions on crash — an attempt is either recorded succeeded/failed after the fact or never started (single-replica assumption). Executions are at-least-once; the idempotency_key is handed to executors so downstream systems can dedupe. skipped is the terminal status for dry_run-mode executions. pending_approval → pending is the approval transition, performed by approval tooling, never by effectd itself.
Trust ladder mirroring ingest: dry_run plans but never executes (executions recorded as skipped), approval requires explicit human transition to pending, auto executes immediately.

Execution lifecycle states. pending: work-queue state ready for an executor to pick up; dead: retries exhausted; failed: non-retryable failure or terminal; skipped: dry_run mode (execution recorded but never performed); succeeded: completed normally.
